heroDesc: Select post-processing by appearance, corrosion protection, wear behavior, conductivity, marking and cleanability.
sectionDesc: Common finishing routes are organized around appearance, corrosion protection, wear behavior, conductivity, traceability and clean stability, with material, masking, thickness, color and inspection needs confirmed before production.

cards.title: Anodizing
cards.p: A common aluminum finish that improves corrosion resistance, wear behavior and appearance options such as clear or black.
cards.body: A common aluminum finish that improves corrosion resistance, wear behavior and appearance options such as clear or black.

cards.title: Electroplating
cards.p: Zinc, nickel, chrome, tin and other plating routes improve corrosion resistance, conductivity, wear or appearance.
cards.body: Zinc, nickel, chrome, tin and other plating routes improve corrosion resistance, conductivity, wear or appearance.

cards.title: Painting
cards.p: Supports color, gloss, insulating protection and masked areas on metal and selected plastic parts.
cards.body: Supports color, gloss, insulating protection and masked areas on metal and selected plastic parts.

cards.title: Powder Coating
cards.p: Good for metal housings, brackets and sheet-metal parts with thicker coating, weather resistance and stable colors.
cards.body: Good for metal housings, brackets and sheet-metal parts with thicker coating, weather resistance and stable colors.

cards.title: Passivation
cards.p: Stabilizes stainless steel surfaces by removing free-iron contamination and improving corrosion performance.
cards.body: Stabilizes stainless steel surfaces by removing free-iron contamination and improving corrosion performance.

cards.title: Heat Treatment
cards.p: Quenching, tempering, aging and related routes improve hardness, strength or dimensional stability.
cards.body: Quenching, tempering, aging and related routes improve hardness, strength or dimensional stability.

cards.title: Laser Marking
cards.p: Permanent serial numbers, QR codes, logos and traceability marks for prototype and batch parts.
cards.body: Permanent serial numbers, QR codes, logos and traceability marks for prototype and batch parts.

cards.title: Polishing
cards.p: Improves surface smoothness, touch and local reflectivity for cosmetic parts, sealing faces and sliding contact areas.
cards.body: Improves surface smoothness, touch and local reflectivity for cosmetic parts, sealing faces and sliding contact areas.

cards.title: Black Oxide
cards.p: Adds a black appearance and basic rust protection for steel fixtures, bushings, transmission and mechanical parts.
cards.body: Adds a black appearance and basic rust protection for steel fixtures, bushings, transmission and mechanical parts.

faq.title: Surface Finishing FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Surface Finishing suitable for?
items.a: Surface Finishing is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Surface Finishing quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Surface Finishing?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share material, appearance, corrosion, hardness, masking and inspection requirements so the route can be reviewed with part geometry.
